summary: Mode-specific GM implementation slice touching seven src/melee/gm files. The slice improved several GM functions and data sections, with reviewer feedback focused on keeping ordinary literals/strings inline rather than promoting them to data symbols or pointer-offset string references. The PR body says slice-specific CI/regression QA was still in progress at intake time, while bot output reported three new matches and multiple unmatched-item improvements.

what_changed: Changed seven mode-specific GM implementation files under src/melee/gm: gmallstar.c, gmcamera.c, gmclassic.c, gmregclear.c, gmresultplayer.c, gmstaffroll.c, and gmtou.c.

PR body frames the work as draft slice 2 of 14 from an aggregate WIP branch, limited to GM mode-specific implementation files and not intended to include header/naming churn beyond implementation needs.
Bot report recorded three new matches: gmregclear fn_8017EE40, gmcamera gmCamera_801A2AAC, and gmcamera gmCamera_801A2BF0.
Bot report recorded improvements across 25 unmatched items including GM .data/.sdata sections and functions in gmstaffroll, gmtou, gmresultplayer, gmallstar, gmcamera, gmclassic, and gmregclear.
Diff excerpt shows cleanup from generated-looking unrolled copy blocks to a simple for loop in gmallstar gm_801B5324.
Diff excerpt shows typed access cleanup in gmallstar by using struct GameCache* and direct entries/stage_id fields instead of nested game_cache accesses through a broader PreloadCacheScene pointer.
Diff excerpt shows gmcamera branch dispatch in gmCamera_801A2AAC reshaped into switch cases.
Review comments requested literal cleanup: inline const floats in gmcamera, inline or restore string literals in gmtou, and replace gmregclear data-offset string references with identified strings.

smart_moves.title: Kept risky validation claims bounded
smart_moves.detail: The PR body distinguishes aggregate WIP validation from slice-specific validation, saying aggregate WIP was clean but slice-specific CI/regression QA was in progress.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification section

smart_moves.title: Dropped gmresult.c from this slice to avoid known .sdata regression
smart_moves.detail: The PR body explicitly says gmresult.c was intentionally not included because it was dropped from the aggregate branch to remove the .sdata regression.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Reviewer Notes

smart_moves.title: Restored only narrow volatile accesses for one matching issue
smart_moves.detail: Author comment says normal field accesses in gm_80182174 caused a nonmatched regression and that restoring only volatile accesses around initialized state fields preserved the match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text issue comment 4711784593

decomp_lessons.lesson: For code-matching PRs, ordinary float constants and asset/resource strings should generally stay inline unless data ownership evidence justifies named storage or pointer-offset access.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418295704 inline these floats
review_comments: discussion_r3418302408 inline these in their uses
review_comments: discussion_r3418304371 put these back

decomp_lessons.lesson: When repeated generated-looking copies differ only by index, a natural counted loop is a supported source-shape candidate.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_801B5324 removes unrolled copy blocks and uses for (count_processed = 0; count_processed < count; count_processed++)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Typed subobject pointers can simplify recovered field access when the diff shows the real substructure, but matching side effects still need verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gc = &lbDvd_8001822C()->game_cache; gc->entries and gc->stage_id used directly

decomp_lessons.lesson: Volatile or access-shape steering should be narrow and evidence-backed; in this PR the author reports restoring only the volatile accesses needed to avoid a specific gm_80182174 nonmatch regression.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text issue comment 4711784593: normal field accesses regressed 96.46% -> 96.39%; narrow volatile accesses preserved match

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pointer temporaries to preserve access/register shape
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmcamera uses px8 and px18 helper access in gmCamera_801A2BF0
diff_excerpt: gmallstar uses chars_ptr and cpu_level pointer locals
human_pr_text issue comment 4711784593: access shape in gm_80182174 affected match percentage
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Supported only as observed matching tactics in this PR.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Control-flow reshaping to switch or goto-labelled loop
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmCamera_801A2AAC range checks converted to grouped switch cases
diff_excerpt: gmClassic_801B2BA4 changed from do/while shape to labelled loop/check flow
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The gmcamera switch aligns with canonical control-flow cleanup; the gmclassic goto shape should not be generalized without more matching evidence.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.interpretation: Do not introduce const f32 symbols for simple 5.0f, -5.0f, 1.0f, or 2.0f values in this code-matching context; keep them inline unless ownership evidence exists.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418295704

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.comment: These "data[n]" entries are strings, can you let the AI figure those out and put them in here?
review_feedback.interpretation: Raw data-offset references to strings in lbArchive_80016DBC should be resolved to readable string literals where possible.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418299197

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.interpretation: Static char objects for ordinary string literals such as ScGamTour_scene_data and SIS_TournamentData should be inlined in uses for this PR.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418302408

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.interpretation: Replace strbase + offsets with the prior readable string literals in lbArchive_80016DBC and HSD_SisLib_803A62A0 calls.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418304371

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inline ordinary literals in code-matching GM work unless data ownership is evidenced
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR2705, reviewer feedback requested inlining simple const floats in gmcamera and restoring/inlining ordinary string literals in gmtou rather than using static char objects or strbase offsets. This supports the existing standards around literals/data ownership and no string-literal symbol regression.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418295704
review_comments: discussion_r3418302408
review_comments: discussion_r3418304371

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow matching tactics need explicit regression evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: The author reported that normal field accesses in gm_80182174 caused a nonmatched regression from 96.46% to 96.39%, and that restoring only volatile accesses around initialized state fields preserved the match. Treat this as narrow tactic evidence, not a general style rule.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text issue comment 4711784593

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR2705 as an example for literal inlining review
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example for literals-and-data-ownership/no-string-literal-symbol-regression: reviewer asked to inline simple float constants in gmCamera_801A2BF0 and to restore ordinary string literals such as ScGamTour_scene_data and SIS_TournamentData instead of static char objects or strbase offsets.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418295704
review_comments: discussion_r3418302408
review_comments: discussion_r3418304371
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover the rule; this PR provides a concrete historical review example for curator consideration.

source_update_candidates.title: GM mode-specific files have recurring literal/data ownership review risk
source_update_candidates.text: PR2705 review comments in src/melee/gm/gmcamera.c, src/melee/gm/gmtou.c, and src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c focused on replacing named simple constants, static char string objects, strbase offsets, and data[n] string offsets with readable inline literals where supported.
evidence_refs: review_comments: discussion_r3418295704
review_comments: discussion_r3418299197
review_comments: discussion_r3418302408
review_comments: discussion_r3418304371
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future reviewers search for literal/data ownership pitfalls in GM files, but should be curated as a path hint rather than a global rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK changes in this PR as accepted source style; the diff shows them but loaded evidence does not include specific objdiff justification per use.
Do not infer final slice-specific CI/regression status from the PR body; it says QA was in progress, though bot output reported improvements.
Do not generalize the gmClassic_801B2BA4 labelled-goto shape as preferred authored style; it appears in the diff as a matching/control-flow tactic only.
Do not claim all review comments were resolved; loaded evidence includes comments but not full final post-review hunks.
